qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Authenticated T3D import decompresses attacker-controlled payloads without size limits
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding targets the import/export module path `Import::loadFile()` -> `Import::getNextFilePart()` in `typo3/sysext/impexp/Classes/Import.php`, while the advisory fixes missing authorization and `tables_modify` checks in the recycler hard-delete flow (`typo3/sysext/recycler/Classes/Controller/RecyclerAjaxController.php` and `typo3/sysext/core/Classes/DataHandling/DataHandler.php`).

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The only final qualifying finding is an authenticated import/export decompression denial-of-service in `typo3/sysext/impexp`, not the recycler broken access control flaw described by GHSA-p52w-7rhw-9m67.
evidence: The advisory summary describes backend users with recycler access deleting arbitrary records from TCA tables because recycler hard-delete operations lacked proper authorization and permission checks.

The advisory fix commits are titled "[SECURITY] Avoid record deletion without permissions in recycler module" and modify recycler deletion handling in `typo3/sysext/recycler/Classes/Controller/RecyclerAjaxController.php` together with permission enforcement in `typo3/sysext/core/Classes/DataHandling/DataHandler.php`.

The only qualifying finding instead traces `POST /record/importexport/import` into `typo3/sysext/impexp/Classes/Import.php`, where attacker-controlled T3D parts are decompressed without size limits and cause PHP memory exhaustion.
